title: Rivian Q1 2024 Earnings Results - Revenue Beat, Earnings Miss
text: Rivian exceeded revenue expectations but disappointed on profitability. The company reported $1.204 billion in revenue versus analyst estimates of $1.175 billion, representing an 82% year-over-year increase. However, losses widened to $1.48 per share compared to the anticipated $1.15 per share. The automaker delivered 13,588 units in Q1, flat from Q4 2023 but up 71% annually. Rivian captured 5.1% market share in US EVs in Q1, a significant achievement for a luxury-focused manufacturer. Gross margin showed modest improvement, with per-vehicle losses declining to $38,784 per vehicle from the prior quarter's $43,372 per vehicle. Cash reserves decreased to $7.858 billion from $9.368 billion at year-end 2023. The company reduced 2024 capital expenditure guidance to $1.2 billion from $1.75 billion and reaffirmed full-year production of 57,000 units while maintaining its $2.7 billion loss projection. Management expects slight positive gross profit in Q4 following a recent plant retooling initiative designed to increase efficiency by approximately 30%.
